brief_title: Clinical Study of Ixabepilone Administered as a 24 Hour Infusion in Patients With Solid Malignancies.
official_title: Phase I Study of Ixabepilone Administered as a 24-Hour Infusion in Patients With Advanced Solid Malignancies
brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to determine the dose limiting toxicities, minimum tolerated dose and recommended dose for Phase II studies.

interventions.name: Ixabepilone
interventions.description: Intravenous (IV) Infusion; 10, 20, 30, 35, 40 \& 45 mg/m2, once every 21 days (1 cycle), up to 9 cycles

eligibility.criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* confirmed diagnosis of a primary solid tumor
* measurable or non-measurable disease
* progressive disease
* men and women greater or equal to 18 years of age.

Exclusion Criteria:

* women of child bearing potential who are not using birth control
* women who are pregnant or breast feeding
* women with a positive pregnancy test on enrollment
* patients with brain metastasis
* prior treatment with Ixabepilone
* known history of human immunodeficiency virus (HIV)

locations.facility: The Cancer Institute Of New Jersey

primary_outcomes.measure: Number of Participants With Dose Limiting Toxicities at Dose Level
primary_outcomes.description: Dose limiting toxicities=any of the following events attributed to Ixabepilone occuring during the first cycle: grade 3/4 nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea despite medical intervention and/or prophylaxis; other Grade ≥3 nonhematological toxicity; any toxicity requiring study therapy discontinuation; delayed recovery from study therapy-related toxicity which delays scheduled re-treatment for \>14 days; Grade 4 neutropenia for ≥5 consecutive days; grade 3/4 neutropenia with sepsis or a fever ≥38.5 C; thrombocytopenia \<25,000 cells/mm3 or bleeding requiring a platelet transfusion.
